label: commit: ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc
prompt:
You are running the commit flow for ponder 'ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc' in the sdlc workspace.

The ponder status has already been marked committed. Your job is to synthesize
the idea into milestones and features.

## Step 1 — Load context

```bash
sdlc ponder show ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc
sdlc ponder artifacts ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc
sdlc ponder session list ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc
```

Read every scrapbook artifact and all session logs (`sdlc ponder session read ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc <N>`) to understand the full idea. Also check existing milestones to avoid duplication:
```bash
sdlc milestone list
sdlc feature list
```

## Step 2 — Size the idea

Based on scope:
- Single concern → one feature, add to an existing milestone if one fits
- Medium idea → one new milestone with 2–5 features
- Large idea → multiple milestones

## Step 3 — Create milestones and features

```bash
sdlc milestone create <slug> --title "<title>" --vision "<one-line why>"
sdlc feature create <slug> --title "<title>"
sdlc milestone add-feature <milestone-slug> <feature-slug>
```

Track every milestone slug you create or update.

## Step 4 — Link committed-to milestones

Record which milestones came from this ponder:
```bash
sdlc ponder update ponder-conversations-need-to-be-more-acc --committed-to <milestone-slug> [--committed-to <milestone-2> ...]
```

## Step 5 — Report

Output a brief summary: what was created, which milestones, suggested next command.
